title: ulmus glabra var. scabra
intro: Ulmus glabra var. scabra, also known as Wych Elm or Scrub Elm, is a variety of elm tree native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive scabrous bark and broad, deciduous leaves. The medicinal properties of Ulmus glabra var. scabra lie in its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain, swelling, and promote wound healing. Ulmus glabra var. scabra can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
